title: pultenaea capitata
intro: Pultenaea capitata, also known as the Golden Pea or Button Bush, is a shrub native to Australia and is a member of the pea family. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Pultenaea capitata include its ability to reduce inflammation, soothe skin irritations, and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as infusions and tinctures, can be made by drying the leaves and stems, and then steeping them in hot water or mixing them with a solvent like ethanol.
